Name:
Before                    Mouth Fulla Gold
(Deep Purple, Purple Swagg)
2008 Freestyle
Notes: An early Rocky freestyle where he is recorded sitting on a bed of some sort. The origins of the freestyle are currently not known, but the timeframe is likely late 2007 to sometime in 2008. This is because Rocky can be seen wearing a limited edition Alife Ball-Out T-Shirt which was released on November 27th, 2007 making 2008 more likely.

Name: 2008 Freestyle
Notes: Freestyle recorded from a webcam in which it's origins are currently unknown, but said to be from 2008. This possibly came from Rocky's cuurrently lost UStream account prior to his fame, but nothing is confirmed. The video linked here is one minute long and partial as the full video is lost as of right now.

Name: 2010 Cypher Freestyle
Notes: On August 28th, 2016 a video was uploaded to YouTube with the title of "Throwback Footage Of A$AP Rocky In A Cypher Back In 2010! Asap Rocky 2016". In this video a pre-fame Rocky can be seen at 3:42 in a Harlem New York freestyle Cypher sometime in 2010.

Name:
2010 Rap Battle
(with A$AP Ferg)
Notes: Video said to have been recorded in 2010 where Rocky and A$AP Ferg, then D-Ferg, freestyle in The Standard Hotel in New York City. Allegedly, Rocky used the lyrics from this rap battle in other freestyles.

Name:
Get High [Music Video]
(feat. A$AP Ferg) (dir. King Zulu)
Notes: Music video for the song "Get High" featuring A$AP Ferg which was directed by King Zulu. The video released on November 23rd, 2010.

Name:
Bittersweet Symphony Part 2 [Music Video]
(dir. Nabil El Hajjouti)
Notes: Music video for the song "Bittersweet Symphony Part 2" which was made during an interview with Press Pause with the video being directed by Nabil El Hajjouti. The video starts out with a quote from the song, then shows Rocky rapping the lyrics spoken word. This transitions into the actual song, but ends before the song can finish. The video uses clips from the "Wassup" music video from Noisey.

Name:
Purple Swag
(dir. Jason Ano & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Purple Swag" which was directed by Jason Ano and A$AP Rocky. The video was released on July 5th, 2011. Towards the end of the music video it cuts into a slowed down version of the track "Peso", then goes back into the actual song. The video features Anna Trill, a friend of the mob at the time, lip-syncing to the song which Rocky specifically wanted a white girl to do.

Name:
Real Shit From A Real Nigga
(feat. Von) (prod. Clams Casino)
Notes: 2011 freestyle over Clams Casino's "Real Shit From A Real Nigga" which was originally by Lil B. Rocky is seen freestyling along with Von. Uploaded September 18th, 2012 onto TheFilmatics YouTube channel and is shot by Jon Filmatic and was edited by Chris Filmatic.

Name:
Peso
(feat. Kevin Andres) (dir. Abteen Bagheri & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Peso" which was directed by Abteen Bagheri and A$AP Rocky. The video released on August 10th, 2011. Totally separate from the "Peso" video interlude shown in the video for "Purple Swag".

Name:
Demons
(dir. Donjai)
Notes: Music video for the song "Demons" which was directed by Donjai. The video leaked online without consent from Rocky on November 29th, 2011. Rocky's response on Twitter to the music video leaking was "THE GARBAGE ASS DEMONS VIDEO THAT LEAKED WAS NOT CLEARED BY US".

Name:
Wassup
(dir. Andy Capper & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Wassup" was directed by Andy Capper and A$AP Rocky. The video released on January 20th, 2012. Rocky teamed up with VICE to make a Scarface, Belly, Enter The Dragon, The Warriors and Willy Wonka and The Chocolate Factory inspired video. The video depicts Rocky going through a dreamy fantasy world filled with the 40s, Ferraris, homies and hoards of cash as the description of the music video states.

Name:
Brand New Guy
(feat. ScHoolboy Q) (dir. The ICU & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Brand New Guy" featuring ScHoolboy Q which was directed by The ICU & A$AP Rocky. The video was released on June 4th, 2012. A "draft" for the music video can be seen in a A$AP Yams Instagram post but the differences are unknown.

Name:
Lil B - ???
(prod. Clams Casino)
(Bass)
Notes: Before Rocky would rap on the Bass instrumental, Clams Casino originally sent the beat to Lil B in 2009 but Lil B would pass on it due to it being "too fast". The beat would later be reworked into Lil B's "I'm The Devil" and Rocky would release "Bass" on LIVE.LOVE.A$AP only 2 years later. Unknown if Lil B actually rapped on the beat before passing it.

Name:
Lord$ Never Worry
(LONG.LIVE.A$AP)
(Collaboration with A$AP Mob)

Told Ya
(with A$AP Ant & Bodega Bamz) (dir. A$AP Ant)
Notes: Music video for the song "Told Ya" for the A$AP Mob's "Lord$ Never Worry" mixtape. Directed by A$AP Ant and released on March 26th, 2012.

Name:
Purple Kisses
(perf. A$AP Rocky) (dir. Luke Monaghan & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Purple Kisses" for the A$AP Mob's "Lord$ Never Worry" mixtape. Directed by Luke Monaghan and A$AP Rocky and released on August 14th, 2012.

Name:

(perf. A$AP Rocky, A$AP Ant & Flatbush Zombies) (dir. Shomi Patwary & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Bath Salt" for the A$AP Mob's "Lord$ Never Worry" mixtape. Directed by Shomi Patwary and A$AP Rocky and released on September 4th, 2012.

Name:
Y.N.R.E.
(with A$AP Twelvyy) (dir. Simon Davis)
Notes: Music video for the song "Y.N.R.E." for the A$AP Mob's "Lord$ Never Worry" mixtape. Directed by Simon Davis. Edited by Simon Davis. Shot by Jason Filmore. Produced by Marcus Williams. The video released on December 12th, 2012.

Name:
Work
(with A$AP Ferg) (dir. A$AP Ferg, Thuan Tran & Shomi Patwary)
Notes: Music video for the song "Work" for the A$AP Mob's "Lord$ Never Worry" mixtape. Directed by A$AP Ferg, Thuan Tran and Shomi Patwary. The video released on January 14th, 2013.

Name:
Jay Reed
(with A$AP Twelvyy & Da$h) (dir. Simon Davis)
Notes: Music video for the song "Jay Reed" for the A$AP Mob's "Lord$ Never Worry" mixtape. Directed by Simon Davis. The video released on April 10th, 2013.

Name:
Persian White
(with A$AP Ferg) (dir. Thuan Tran & A$AP Ferg)
Notes: Music video for the song "Persian White" for the A$AP Mob's "Lord$ Never Worry" mixtape. Directed by Thuan Tran and A$AP Ferg. Photography directed by Thuan Tran and Justin Hogan. Edited by Thuan Tran. Produced by George Fernandez and anthony Blue. Sound design by Richie Batista. Colors by Thai Tran. Effects by Thai Tran. The video released on April 23rd, 2013.

Name:
Pumped Up Kicks
(prod. Mark Foster)
Notes: A cover of Foster The People's song "Pumped Up Kicks" which changes some of the lyrics. During a session at BBC Radio 1's Maida Vale studios in London for Zane Lowe's show on August 1st, 2012. The song features alternate lyrics from the real Foster The People version.

Name:
Real Shit From A Real Nigga
(feat. Von) (prod. Clams Casino)
Notes: Freestyle over Clams Casino's "Real Shit From A Real Nigga" which was originally by Lil B. Rocky is seen freestyling along with Von. Uploaded September 18th, 2012 onto TheFilmatics YouTube channel and is shot by Jon Filmatic and was edited by Chris Filmatic.

Name:
Goldie
(dir. A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Goldie" which was directed by A$AP Rocky. The video released on May 8th, 2012.

Notes: Music video for the song "Fuckin' Problems" featuring Drake, Kendrick Lamar and 2 Chainz was directed by Sam Lecca and Clark Jackson. The video was released on December 4th, 2012.

Name:
Long Live A$AP
(dir. Samantha Lecca & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Long Live A$AP" was directed by Samantha Lecca and A$AP Rocky. The video was released on December 24th, 2012.

Name:
Wild for the Night

Name:

Name:
Travis Scott - Quintana
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (prod. Sak Pase, Travis Scott & anthony Kilhoffer)
Notes: The original version of Travis Scott's "Quintana" was meant to feature Rocky. According to a notorious leaker by the name of Pharoahxx, Rocky was meant to record for the Owl Phaorah track but passed out in the studio off lean so A$AP Bari recorded instead.

Hella Hoes
(perf. A$AP Rocky, A$AP Nast, A$AP Twelvyy & A$AP Ferg) (dir. )
Notes: Music video for "Hella Hoes".

Name: Funk Flex Freestyle
Notes: Freestyle on Funk Flex with A$AP Mob were they freestyle over the instrumental to "Who Shot Ya?" by The Notourious B.I.G.

Name:
SVDDXNLY [Documentary]
(dir. David Laven)
Notes: Five part documentary series about Rocky's upbringing by Noisy.

Name:
Drake - Wu-Tang Forever (Remix)
(feat. A$AP Rocky) (dir. A$AP Rocky & Jessy Terrero)
Notes: Photos of a music video for Drake's song "Wu-Tang Forever" surfaced with Rocky making an appearance surfaced in January 2014. This made many rumors for years to come that Rocky was on the song. In 2016 an official remix came out with a Rocky verse but, this music video hasn't been released to this day.

Name:
Multiply
(dir. Shomi Patwary, A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Multiply" released on __ and during the music video a snippet for "Lord Pretty Flacko Jodye 2 (LPFJ2)" is played. Directed by Shomi Patwary and A$AP Rocky. Photography directed by Thuan Tran and Sebastian Jimenez. Produced by Chad Gihron and Sameer Naseem. Editing and post by Shomi Patwary and Punom Patwary. First AD by Stephanie Lou Hague. Casting by Jorge Wright and Booki Powell. Wardrobe by Ling and Matthew Henson. PA by Ryan Cagide, Van Young and Tashfiq Patwary.

Name:
Lord Pretty Flacko Jodye 2 (LPFJ2)
(dir. Samantha Lecca)
Notes: Music video for the song "Lord Pretty Flacko Jodye 2 (LPFJ2)" released on February 11th, 2015 as a tribute to A$AP Yams who passed away just a month before the videos release. Directed by Samantha Lecca.

Name:
L$D
(dir. Dexter Navy & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "L$D" released on May 19th, 2015 with the title "L$D (LOVE x $EX x DREAMS)" and is heavily inspired by Gaspar Noe's 2009 psychedelic art film "Enter the Void". The second half of the video features a snippet for the song "Excuse Me". Directed by Dexter Navy and A$AP Rocky.

Name:
Jukebox Joints
(feat. Joe Fox) (dir. Shomi Patwary & A$AP Rocky)
Notes: Music video for the song "Jukebox Joints" was released on August 11th, 2015 and notably doesn't feature any vocals from Kanye West. Directed by Shomi Patwary and A$AP Rocky. Executive produced by A$AP Rocky and Chase Infinite. Photography directed by Thuan Tran. Produced by Chad Ghiron with Kode Media. Styling by Matthew A. Henson and Isamar Parache. Edited by Shomi Patwary and Tanzina Alam Patwary. Colors by Robert Simmons. Mary Attaway as art director. Motion design by Robert Simmons. Sebastian Jimenez as camera assistant. Booki Powell, Even Neuhausen, A$AP Lou, Wafiqu Chowdhury and Tash Patwary as assistant producers. Ken Shaffer as gaffer. Colin Kelly as best boy. Casting by Booki Powell, Tashfiq Patwary, Arelie Gutierrez and Gitoo. Make up by Erika B. and Cati James. Commissioned by Akila Robinson. Dame Lavie, Marquiz Tibbz, Kamil Boguski, Ryan Cotham, Keys Kravitz, Kate Macdougall, Jenn Levy, Matt McDonald, Daniel Cook and Mike Garcia. Video recording locations include TU Barrio Barbershop and Soho Arts Club. Sound design by Héctor Delgado. Intro produced by Stefanie Soho. Director of photography Marcus Gelhard. Steadycam by Joey Roesgen. Sound by Alexander Stark. 1st ad by Martin Kuehner. Co-Produced by Kyra Sophie. Set design by Chakir Hani. Styling by Dogukan Nesanir. Victoria Maschuw, Dinah Konyaté, Kyra Sophie, Alyssa Cordes, Gianna Petrovic and Juliette Mainx are women that appear in the video.

Name:
Everyday
(dir. Emmanuel Cossu & Fleaur & Manu)
Notes: Music video for the song "Everyday" was released on November 24th, 2015 and is described as a hip-hop Hollywood story. Directed by Emmanuel Cossu and Fleaur & Manu. Alex Courtes as 2nd unit director. Division as the production company. Executive produced by Jules De Chateleux.
